comments.body: @vrothberg @rhatdan what did we do on podman/buildah for the docker hub rate limiting issue, or is that yet to be solved?
@lsm5, @edsantiago worked his registries.conf magic as outlined in this blog:  https://www.redhat.com/sysadmin/container-quayio

> @lsm5, @edsantiago worked his registries.conf magic as outlined in this blog: https://www.redhat.com/sysadmin/container-quayio

So, openshift has a `origin-hello-openshift` on quay.io. Hopefully, that should Just Work:tm:
LGTM, but I'd like @vrothberg to weigh in tomorrow before merging.  I know in the past he's preferred to use a particular version tag rather than latest.

> LGTM, but I'd like @vrothberg to weigh in tomorrow before merging. I know in the past he's preferred to use a particular version tag rather than latest.

Hmm, I'm not opposed to it, but I'd say `fedora:latest` is pretty stable, and this way we'd not need a change every new release. Either way..

> @vrothberg @rhatdan what did we do on podman/buildah for the docker hub rate limiting issue, or is that yet to be solved?

Yet to be solved.  I want us to tackle that next week when bug sprint is over.  IMHO a sick CI counts as a bug.

> LGTM, but I'd like @vrothberg to weigh in tomorrow before merging. I know in the past he's preferred to use a particular version tag rather than latest.

Thanks!  I prefer using non-latest tags.  Using "latest" is asking for trouble as it's deliberately a moving target.  Not sure if, for instance, the recent glibc-seccomp fart was introduced to latest but I also don't want to find out :^)

@lsm5, could you use f33 instead?

> @lsm5, could you use f33 instead?

Alright, changing ..

LGTM
Merge once the tests pass.
